rules.consumes_emits_discipline: Consumes/emits alone must not create hard_dependency unless phase-doc Required dependencies, business logic, ADL, activation/lifecycle, billing/evidence, or manual decision provides a hard basis.
rules.correct_example: 6A.08 hard-depends on 6A.07 because 6A.07 is in Required dependencies; consumes/emits only supports the reason.
rules.incorrect_example: Do not infer Audit Log hard-depends on Event Bus merely from emitted/consumed evidence elsewhere.
rules.foundry_manifest_dependency_target: service_manifest_contract is the default manifest traceability target for activatable/configurable service-like Phase 6A boundaries.
rules.adl_004_scope: ADL-004 is Phase 6A relevant through Communication Gateway / Global Opt-Out.
rules.adl_016_scope: ADL-016 is deferred_to_phase_6b_gl and is not represented as a Phase 6A seed or sub-surface.

6A.12 requires 6A.08; accepted API writes must publish through event delivery mechanics.
API key scope registry is tenant-configurable and traces to service_manifest_contract.
6A.12 requires 6A.08; idempotency key management must coordinate accepted writes with event delivery mechanics.
Provider-specific adapters are later implementations; Phase 6A records API/webhook registries, validation, idempotency, retry, and rejection boundaries only.
Webhook definitions are scoped by API key/capability registry before inbound validation is accepted.
Webhook definition registry is provider-facing configuration and traces to service_manifest_contract.
Inbound webhook validation requires idempotency key management before deduplicating provider retries.
Inbound webhook validation is provider-facing boundary behavior and traces to service_manifest_contract.
Inbound webhook validation requires registered webhook definitions before accepting or rejecting provider traffic.
Webhook retry schedule depends on idempotency keys to avoid duplicate provider retry processing.
Webhook retry schedule is only meaningful after inbound webhook validation classifies accepted/rejected delivery attempts.
6A.12 requires 6A.09; delivery rejection evidence must remain billable/measurable where provider traffic is priced.
Delivery rejection logs are emitted from inbound webhook validation outcomes.
